title: Mercedes Lackey

infobox.value: Anthony Lackey  ​  ​ (m. 1972; div. 1990)​      [Larry Dixon](/wiki/Larry_Dixon_(fantasy_artist) "Larry Dixon (fantasy artist)")  ​ (m. 1990)​ [[1]](#cite_note-pringle-1)

(born June 24, 1950) is an American writer of [fantasy novels](/wiki/Fantasy_literature "Fantasy literature"). Many of her novels and trilogies are interlinked and set in the world of [Velgarth](/wiki/Velgarth "Velgarth"), mostly in and around the country of [Valdemar](/wiki/Valdemar_(fictional_country) "Valdemar (fictional country)"). Her Valdemar novels include interaction between human and non-human protagonists with many different cultures and social [mores](/wiki/Mores "Mores").

from the article and its talk page, especially if potentially [libelous](/wiki/Wikipedia:Libel "Wikipedia:Libel"). *Find sources:* ["Mercedes Lackey"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2021)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American writer of fantasy novels (born 1950)

| Mercedes Lackey | |
| --- | --- |
| [Lackey at the 2018 Phoenix Comic Fest](/wiki/File:Mercedes_Lackey_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg "Lackey at the 2018 Phoenix Comic Fest") Lackey at the 2018 Phoenix Comic Fest | |
| Born | (1950-06-24) June 24, 1950 (age 75)[[1]](#cite_note-pringle-1)  [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois"), U.S. |
| Occupation | Novelist, short story writer |
| Alma mater | [Purdue University](/wiki/Purdue_University "Purdue University") |
| Period | 1985–present |
| Genre | [Fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy "Fantasy") |
| Notable works | Valdemar series |
| Spouse | Anthony Lackey  ​  ​ (m. 1972; div. 1990)​      [Larry Dixon](/wiki/Larry_Dixon_(fantasy_artist) "Larry Dixon (fantasy artist)")  ​ (m. 1990)​ [[1]](#cite_note-pringle-1) |
| Website | |
| [mercedeslackey.com](http://mercedeslackey.com) | |

**Mercedes Ritchie Lackey** (born June 24, 1950) is an American writer of [fantasy novels](/wiki/Fantasy_literature "Fantasy literature"). Many of her novels and trilogies are interlinked and set in the world of [Velgarth](/wiki/Velgarth "Velgarth"), mostly in and around the country of [Valdemar](/wiki/Valdemar_(fictional_country) "Valdemar (fictional country)"). Her Valdemar novels include interaction between human and non-human protagonists with many different cultures and social [mores](/wiki/Mores "Mores").

Her other main world is similar to Earth, but it includes clandestine populations of elves, mages, vampires, and other mythical beings. The *Bedlam's Bard* books describe a young man with the power to work magic through music; the *SERRAted Edge* books are about racecar driving elves; and the *Diana Tregarde* thrillers center on a [Wiccan](/wiki/Wiccan "Wiccan") who combats evil.

She has also published several novels re-working well-known fairy tales set in a mid-19th to early 20th century setting in which magic is real, although hidden from the mundane world. These novels explore issues of ecology, social class, and gender roles.

Lackey has published over 140 books and writes novels at a rate of 5.5 per year on average. She has been called one of the "most prolific science fiction and fantasy writers of all time."[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 2021, Lackey was named the 38th [Damon Knight Grand Master](/wiki/Damon_Knight_Memorial_Grand_Master_Award "Damon Knight Memorial Grand Master Award").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mercedes_Lackey&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Lackey was born in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago").[[1]](#cite_note-pringle-1) Her birth prevented her father from being called to serve in the [Korean War](/wiki/Korean_War "Korean War").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

She placed her meeting with [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") at age 10 or 11, when she picked up her father's copy of [James H. Schmitz](/wiki/James_H._Schmitz "James H. Schmitz")'s *[Agent of Vega](/wiki/Agent_of_Vega "Agent of Vega")*. She then read [Andre Norton](/wiki/Andre_Norton "Andre Norton")'s *[The Beast Master](/wiki/The_Beast_Master "The Beast Master")* and *Lord of Thunder* and continued to read all of Norton's works. Lackey had difficulties obtaining enough interesting books from the public library to sate her passion for reading. She wrote for herself but without real direction or purpose until she attended [Purdue University](/wiki/Purdue_University "Purdue University"). Lackey graduated from Purdue in 1972.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

While at Purdue, she took a one-on-one class of English Literature Independent Studies with a professor who was a fellow [science fiction fan](/wiki/Science_fiction_fandom "Science fiction fandom"). He helped her analyze books she enjoyed and then use that knowledge. Lackey then encountered [fan fiction](/wiki/Fan_fiction "Fan fiction"), which further encouraged her writing. She began publishing work in [science fiction fanzines](/wiki/Science_fiction_fanzines "Science fiction fanzines") and then discovered [filk](/wiki/Filk "Filk") and had some filk lyrics published by [Off Centaur Publications](/wiki/Off_Centaur_Publications "Off Centaur Publications").

## Professional sales

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mercedes_Lackey&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Professional sales")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e3/Mercedes_Lackey_1a.jpg/250px-Mercedes_Lackey_1a.jpg)](/wiki/File:Mercedes_Lackey_1a.jpg)

Lackey signing autographs at [CONvergence](/wiki/CONvergence "CONvergence"), 2008

Lackey submitted a story to *[Sword and Sorceress](/wiki/Sword_and_Sorceress_series "Sword and Sorceress series")*, then sold the rewritten story to *Fantasy Book Magazine*. Her first sale was to *Friends of Darkover*.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

She met [C. J. Cherryh](/wiki/C._J._Cherryh "C. J. Cherryh") through [filk](/wiki/Filk_music "Filk music"). Cherryh helped Lackey through 17 rewrites of 'Arrows'. During this time, [Marion Zimmer Bradley](/wiki/Marion_Zimmer_Bradley "Marion Zimmer Bradley") included Lackey's short stories in an anthology, *Friends of Darkover*. Lackey claims to have been writing so much during this time that she had no social life at all. She divorced Tony Lackey, and eventually married Larry Dixon.[[7]](#cite_note-FantasticFiction-7)

### Stance on fanfiction

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mercedes_Lackey&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Stance on fanfiction")]

Despite getting her own start as a fanfiction author, she and her agent forbade fan fiction based on her own books for many years, whether distributed offline or online. Lackey stated on her website[[8]](#cite_note-8) that this was due to the 1992 Marion Zimmer Bradley fan fiction affair, when a fan accused Bradley of copying the fan's work and demanded writing credit and remuneration.[[9]](#cite_note-9) After several years, Lackey's policy permitted offline fanfic, but only if the author got a release form from Mercedes Lackey that said the author acknowledged that they were using characters that belong to Mercedes Lackey and that the author's work essentially became Mercedes Lackey's property to prevent "infringing on my right to make a living from my own imagination". As of 2009 this policy changed, permitting fan fiction to be licensed as derivative fiction under the [Creative Commons](/wiki/Creative_Commons "Creative Commons") umbrella.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mercedes_Lackey&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Personal life")]

She married Anthony Lackey in 1972, and they divorced in 1990. In 1990, she met [Larry Dixon](/wiki/Larry_Dixon_(fantasy_artist) "Larry Dixon (fantasy artist)") at Circle Ouroboros convention in Meridian, MS. They married in 1992. [[1]](#cite_note-pringle-1) The couple live outside of [Tulsa, Oklahoma](/wiki/Tulsa,_Oklahoma "Tulsa, Oklahoma").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

### Other interests

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mercedes_Lackey&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Other interests")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This section of a [biography of a living person](/wiki/Wikipedia:Biographies_of_living_persons "Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons") **needs additional [citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help by adding [reliable sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources"). [Contentious material](/wiki/Wikipedia:Biographies_of_living_persons#Remove_contentious_material_that_is_unsourced_or_poorly_sourced "Wikipedia:Biographies of living persons") about living persons that is unsourced or [poorly sourced](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources#Questionable_and_self-published_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources") **must be removed immediately** from the article and its talk page, especially if potentially [libelous](/wiki/Wikipedia:Libel "Wikipedia:Libel"). *Find sources:* ["Mercedes Lackey"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Mercedes+Lackey%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2021)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Lackey and Dixon have in the past worked in [raptor rehabilitation](/wiki/Raptor_rehabilitation "Raptor rehabilitation"). She refers to her various [parrots](/wiki/Parrot "Parrot") as her "feathered children". The afterwords to some of her books refer to rehabilitation and [falconry](/wiki/Falconry "Falconry"), and this interest has influenced and informed her writing. She also enjoys [beadwork](/wiki/Beadwork "Beadwork"), costuming, and [needlework](/wiki/Needlework "Needlework"). She claims, however, to be a "wretched housekeeper, and by and large an indifferent cook"[[13]](#cite_note-13) Besides this, she does radar-reading during tornado season. She helps support the [Alex Foundation](/wiki/Alex_Foundation "Alex Foundation").

Historically, Lackey was active in the [filking](/wiki/Filk "Filk") community. She was a major contributor to an early album of space filk, *[Minus Ten and Counting](/wiki/Minus_Ten_and_Counting "Minus Ten and Counting")*. She has won five [Pegasus Awards](/wiki/Pegasus_Award "Pegasus Award"), mostly for her songwriting.[[14]](#cite_note-14) She was also active in the [Society for Creative Anachronism](/wiki/Society_for_Creative_Anachronism "Society for Creative Anachronism") and belongs to the [Great Dark Horde](/wiki/Great_Dark_Horde "Great Dark Horde").

She participates in The Stellar Guild series published by Phoenix Pick. The series pairs bestselling authors like Lackey with lesser known authors in science fiction and fantasy to help provide additional visibility to them.

## Related writers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mercedes_Lackey&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Related writers")]

Mercedes Lackey was a protégée of [Marion Zimmer Bradley](/wiki/Marion_Zimmer_Bradley "Marion Zimmer Bradley") and got her start writing short stories in Bradley's story collections. Other mentors include writers C. J. Cherryh and [Andre Norton](/wiki/Andre_Norton "Andre Norton"), along with her editor, [Elizabeth (Betsy) Wollheim](/wiki/Betsy_Wollheim "Betsy Wollheim") of [DAW Books](/wiki/DAW_Books "DAW Books").

Her earlier Velgarth novels are all solo projects, but later volumes in the Valdemar saga are illustrated by her husband, Larry Dixon, and in many of her latest works he is also credited as co-author. Many of her other novels are collaborations. She has worked with fantasy authors Andre Norton (such as the Halfblood Chronicles), Marion Zimmer Bradley (such as *Rediscovery* and *Tigers Burning Bright*), [Anne McCaffrey](/wiki/Anne_McCaffrey "Anne McCaffrey") (such as *[The Ship Who Searched](/wiki/The_Ship_Who_Searched "The Ship Who Searched")*), and [Piers Anthony](/wiki/Piers_Anthony "Piers Anthony") (*If I Pay Thee Not in Gold*). She has most recently written *The Obsidian Trilogy* with historian [James Mallory](/w/index.php?title=James_Mallory_(author)&action=edit&redlink=1 "James Mallory (author) (page does not exist)"), a [historical fantasy](/wiki/Historical_fantasy "Historical fantasy") series about an "alternative" [Elizabeth I](/wiki/Elizabeth_I "Elizabeth I") with romance writer [Roberta Gellis](/wiki/Roberta_Gellis "Roberta Gellis"), and the *[Heirs of Alexandria series](/wiki/Heirs_of_Alexandria_series "Heirs of Alexandria series")* with [Dave Freer](/wiki/Dave_Freer "Dave Freer") and [Eric Flint](/wiki/Eric_Flint "Eric Flint").[[7]](#cite_note-FantasticFiction-7)

## Adaptations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mercedes_Lackey&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Adaptations")]

### Television and film

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mercedes_Lackey&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Television and film")]

On August 3, 2021, [Deadline](/wiki/Deadline_Hollywood "Deadline Hollywood") reported that Radar Pictures had acquired the television rights to the Valdemar novels and that [Ted Field](/wiki/Ted_Field "Ted Field") would be Executive Producer on the series. The first season is being developed as an adaptation of the [Last Herald-Mage trilogy](/wiki/Last_Herald_Mage_trilogy "Last Herald Mage trilogy"). [Kit Williamson](/wiki/Kit_Williamson "Kit Williamson") and Brittany Cavallaro have been announced as writers and producers for the show.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

On October 8, 2023, Kit Williamson shared that this adaptation was "now "on hold," which is industry speak for dead" in a members-only post on his Patreon.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Bibliography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mercedes_Lackey&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Bibliography")]

Main article: [Mercedes Lackey bibliography](/wiki/Mercedes_Lackey_bibliography "Mercedes Lackey bibliography")
